key_themes: High, reliable dividend yield (~6.3%) and long history of increases as a 'Dividend King'
Stable 2026 EPS guidance (2.5–5.5% growth to $5.56–$5.72) supported by pricing power despite cigarette volume declines
Broad analyst Buy consensus with recent price target raises (average ~$66, highs to $74)
Long-term risks from secular cigarette decline and mixed success in smoke-free/next-gen products (e.g., on! nicotine pouches)
Strong appeal to income and dividend-growth investors, with some preference for peers like BTI/PM for better international or oral nicotine exposure
summary: Altria (MO) carries solidly positive sentiment among income investors due to its attractive ~6.3% dividend yield, consistent quarterly payouts ($1.06), and 2026 EPS growth guidance that relies on pricing discipline even as traditional cigarette volumes decline. Analysts remain mostly bullish with recent PT upgrades and Buy ratings, while upcoming Q1 results (April 30) are anticipated with cautious optimism. Social and Reddit discussions celebrate its long dividend track record and cash-flow reliability, though some voices note ethical concerns around tobacco and favor competitors with stronger smoke-free portfolios for the long term.[[1]](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/03/31/the-major-long-term-risk-facing-altria-stock-in-20/)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/MO/forecast/)[[3]](https://www.reddit.com/r/dividends/comments/1sjcgwa/altria_group_mo/)
sources: 15+ recent news/articles from Motley Fool, Yahoo Finance, Zacks, company releases, analyst notes (BofA, UBS, etc.), 20+ X posts (semantic + keyword searches since Mar 2026), Multiple Reddit threads (r/dividends, r/Altria) discussing holdings and sentiment
